name: Agent MCP Catalog
description: MCP servers you can plug into a Recomputable Agent, each with its per-tool verification lane derived from the live conformance gate. A tool is 'recomputable' only when a hash-pinned golden-vector suite can re-derive its output from public rules; otherwise it is honestly 'attested'. Don't trust. Recompute.
howToAdd: Gateway admin -> /admin/mcps -> Add MCP server (name, PUBLIC https endpoint, auth). Assign per-agent. Every call flows through the attestation pipeline; tools with a live recipe grade Recomputable on the conformance page, the rest list Attested.

ens_write.v0.rule: calldata = resolver-fn selector || namehash(name) || abi.encode(args); independently derived from EIP-137 + public resolver ABI, no live read

chronicle_checkpoint_continuity.v0.rule: pairwise checkpoint-continuity gate; two independently-authored implementations, source closed, 20/20; recorded in trustless-ai/recompute-kit

communication_chain.v0.rule: Who / Said what / Through which channel / To whom / With what effect — narrative walk; the 'effect' link recomputes from a LIVE ENS MCP call

storage-root.v0.rule: 0G Storage rootHash = flow-merkle over 256B keccak-leaf chunks (flow-pad, odd-node carry); a second independent Python impl (mcp/zerog_merkle.py), cross-checked vs the 0G SDK golden vectors + the live MCP

id-write.v0.rule: setContenthash selector || namehash(name) || abi(bytes ENSIP-7 contenthash(CID)); the CID->0xe301… multicodec encoding is a second independent Python impl (mcp/ens_contenthash.py), cross-checked vs the content-hash library golden vectors + the live MCP

dex-calldata.v0.rule: exactInputSingle selector (0x04e45aaf) || abi(tokenIn,tokenOut,fee,recipient,amountIn,amountOutMinimum,sqrtPriceLimitX96); plain ABI, so cast is the independent reference. Grades a PURE encoder (explicit amountOutMinimum), not the live-quote swap

nft-fulfill.v0.rule: Seaport fulfillBasicOrder selector (0xfb0f3ee1) or _efficient_6GL6yc (0x00000000) || abi(BasicOrderParameters); plain ABI, cast is the independent reference. Grades a PURE encoder from explicit order params, not the live OpenSea fetch. Vectors captured from a real listings/fulfillment_data payload

lanes.recomputable: output re-derived end-to-end from a hash-pinned golden-vector suite, no human in the loop (green badge)
lanes.attested: live tool with no recipe yet — vouched for, not recomputable (amber badge)
lanes.candidate: attested today, but the output is deterministic so a recompute recipe is FEASIBLE and planned (amber badge, roadmap tag)

mcps.name: ENS
mcps.summary: The first ENS *write* MCP: check availability + price, register a .eth name (commit/reveal), and set records (text / addr / primary / contenthash). Non-custodial calldata the user's own wallet signs. All four record-setters are recomputable today — text/addr/primary against ens_write.v0, contenthash against id-write.v0.
mcps.example: Set the url text record for gobross.eth and point its contenthash at ipfs://<cid> (calldata for my wallet to sign).

mcps.name: Uniswap
mcps.summary: Direct Uniswap v3: QuoterV2 price + SwapRouter02 exactInputSingle calldata the user's own wallet signs. Not an aggregator. uniswap_encode_swap is the PURE encoder (explicit amountOutMinimum) — recomputable against dex-calldata.v0. The quote and the quote-and-swap are live pool reads (attested).
mcps.example: Encode a WETH->USDC exactInputSingle with an explicit minOut (uniswap_encode_swap), or quote it live first (uniswap_quote).

tools.name: uniswap_quote
tools.reason: live QuoterV2 on-chain read; price varies with pool state

tools.name: uniswap_swap_calldata
tools.reason: derives amountOutMinimum from a live quote; re-encode via uniswap_encode_swap to recompute the calldata byte-for-byte

mcps.name: 0G
mcps.summary: Stores/fetches an action's recompute artifacts on 0G Storage. og_root returns the pure content-addressed rootHash WITHOUT uploading — recomputable against storage-root.v0 by a second independent flow-merkle implementation. og_store performs the live upload (an action); og_fetch is a content-addressed read.
mcps.example: Compute the 0G root of this artifact (og_root), then store it and return the same rootHash.

tools.name: og_root
tools.recompute: 0G flow-merkle rootHash(content): 256B chunks, keccak256 leaves, flow-pad, odd-node carry

tools.name: og_store_artifact
tools.reason: live upload (gas + network) = an action; its returned rootHash equals storage-root.v0 and is recomputable offline via og_root

tools.name: og_fetch_artifact
tools.reason: content-addressed read; verify-only — fetched bytes must hash back to the requested root

mcps.name: OpenSea
mcps.summary: Reads collection stats, floor prices and live listings, and prepares Seaport buy calldata for real NFTs. opensea_encode_fulfillment is the PURE encoder (explicit order params) — recomputable against nft-fulfill.v0. The reads and the live-order buy are attested.
mcps.example: Encode Seaport fulfillBasicOrder calldata from an explicit order (opensea_encode_fulfillment), or fetch a live listing to buy (opensea_buy_nft).

tools.name: opensea_buy_nft
tools.reason: fetches a live order from the OpenSea API; re-encode its {function, parameters} via opensea_encode_fulfillment to recompute the calldata byte-for-byte

mcps.name: LI.FI
mcps.summary: Finds the best bridge-and-swap route across 30+ chains and returns a ready-to-sign quote. The route depends on live cross-chain liquidity, so it is attested, not recomputable.
mcps.example: Find the best route to bridge 0.1 ETH from Ethereum to Base.

mcps.name: Flashbots
mcps.summary: Private-mempool protection: submits transactions privately, simulates bundles and checks status, shielding trades from front-running and sandwich MEV. Execution/network-bound, so attested.
mcps.example: Check the latest Ethereum block and its base fee.

mcps.name: Alchemy
mcps.summary: On-chain data suite (~97 tools) — token prices, NFT holdings, transfers and full transaction history across 100+ chains. All live reads (attested). Its tools/list is not exposed in the MCP-standard shape the gate introspects, so it is graded at the MCP level, not per tool.
mcps.example: Fetch the current USD price of ETH and its 24h change.
mcps.introspectNote: POST /conformance/introspect returns HTTP 400 — tools/list not in the standard shape; all tools are live reads = attested

mcps.name: Recompute Kit
mcps.summary: Not graded — it IS the recomputer. Re-derives a claim from the primary artifact at a pinned ref, runs golden-vector suites, and emits portable receiptos.evidence_capsule.v0 receipts. This is the engine the whole catalog's lanes come from.
mcps.example: Recompute this on-chain attestation from its primary artifact and show the evidence.

mcps.name: Conformance / Verify
mcps.tagline: Grade any MCP against a hash-pinned suite
mcps.summary: The machine gate as a callable verb (conformance_run) plus a self-service /conformance/introspect endpoint: point it at any MCP, it tools/list's it, matches each tool to the recipe registry, grades the matches against the independent rule-derivation, and returns a tri-state verdict + a portable receipt. Live — this is what powers this catalog.
mcps.example: conformance_run(suite: ens_write.v0, endpoint) -> 5/5 reproduced -> Recomputable badge + downloadable receipt

mcps.name: The Graph
mcps.summary: Queries live blockchain data via subgraphs on The Graph's decentralized network. graph_query reads the latest block (live → attested); graph_query_at_block pins the read to a finalized block — byte-reproducible across indexers, so anyone can independently recompute it. The recomputable lane for indexed data.
mcps.example: Read the Uniswap v3 USDC/WETH pool totalValueLockedUSD at block 20000000 — a byte-reproducible read.

shipped.note: 0G flow-merkle root reimplemented independently in Python, cross-checked vs the SDK's golden vectors + the live MCP; grades Recomputable
ENSIP-7 CID->contenthash reimplemented independently in Python, cross-checked vs the content-hash library golden vectors + the live MCP; grades Recomputable
the pure-variant move: a new read-only encoder with explicit amountOutMinimum, so exactInputSingle calldata is deterministic and cast-reproducible; the live-quote uniswap_swap_calldata stays Attested

the pure-variant move for Seaport: a read-only encoder from explicit BasicOrderParameters, validated byte-for-byte (1674 chars) vs a real fulfillment_data payload + the live opensea_buy_nft; the live-order fetch stays Attested

roadmap.note: Every recomputable-in-principle tool has a shipped recipe — the candidate list is empty. Three patterns, one registry: (1) CUSTOM encoding -> a from-spec impl validated vs published golden vectors (0G flow-merkle, ENSIP-7 multicodec); (2) plain ABI -> cast is the independent reference for free; (3) MIXED deterministic+live -> expose a pure read-only variant, then it's case 2 (Uniswap encode_swap, OpenSea encode_fulfillment). Live-routing/execution/data-read tools (LI.FI routes, Flashbots submits, Alchemy reads) stay Attested by nature — no derivation exists to recompute.

conformance.model: Listing is a recomputable predicate, not a permission. Humans ratify a category's golden-vector suite once; after that a submission is graded machine-only.
conformance.suitePinning: Each suite is content-hashed (SHA-256 over committed blob bytes); a submission is graded against the hash, and a mismatch is unverifiable, never a silent pass.
conformance.provenOn: chronicle_checkpoint_continuity.v0 — two independently-authored implementations, source closed, 20/20; recorded in trustless-ai/recompute-kit.
conformance.machineGate: hash-pinned suite -> independent recomputation -> tri-state verdict -> portable receipt
